perturbation.original_text: Upon termination or expiration of this Agreement, the receiving party agrees to promptly return or destroy, as instructed by the disclosing party, any Proprietary Information received from th e disclosing party, together with all copies thereof, upon request by the disclosing party, termination NDA_English Page 3 of 4 of the Agreement or expiry or termination of the Purpose, whichever is earlier. The recipient shall provide to the disclosing party, within ten (10) day s of such request, expiry or termination, a certificate of one of its authorized senior corporate officer attesting to this return or destruction.
perturbation.changed_text: Upon termination or expiration of this Agreement, the receiving party agrees to promptly return or destroy, as instructed by the disclosing party, any Proprietary Information received from th e disclosing party, together with all copies thereof, upon request by the disclosing party, termination NDA_English Page 3 of 4 of the Agreement or expiry or termination of the Purpose, whichever is earlier. The recipient shall provide to the disclosing party, within thirty (30) day s of such request, expiry or termination, a certificate of one of its authorized senior corporate officer attesting to this return or destruction.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'ten (10) days' to 'thirty (30) days' for providing a certificate of destruction creates an in-text contradiction, as the opening statement requires prompt return/destruction. This creates ambiguity and uncertainty over the time allowed for compliance.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Notwithstanding any termination or expiration of this Agreement, the provisions of this Agreement, as they relate to the safeguard, disclosure or use of Proprietary Information shall, unless agreed otherwise by the Parties, or unless such information becomes part of the public domain through no fault of the receiving party, remain in full force and effect a) for a period of five (5) years from expiration or termination of this Agreement when the disclosing party is a potential supplier to CAE, o r b), for a period of twenty five years if the disclosing party is CAE . Upon termination or expiration of this Agreement, the receiving party agrees to promptly return or destroy, as instructed by the disclosing party, any Proprietary Information received from th e disclosing party, together with all copies thereof, upon request by the disclosing party, termination NDA_English Page 3 of 4 of the Agreement or expiry or termination of the Purpose, whichever is earlier. The recipient shall provide to the disclosing party, within thirty (30) day s of such request, expiry or termination, a certificate of one of its authorized senior corporate officer attesting to this return or destruction. Notwithstanding the foregoing, one (1) copy may be retained in confidential restricted access files of the r ecipient’s legal department for use by its legal counsel strictly in the event of a dispute.
perturbation.justification: The original text specified 'ten (10) days' for providing a certificate of destruction. The changed text alters this to 'thirty (30) days'. The perturbation explanation highlights that this creates an in-text contradiction because the agreement initially requires 'prompt' return/destruction, and extending the certificate timeframe creates ambiguity regarding what constitutes 'prompt' compliance. The contradicted text includes the modified thirty day period. Therefore, based on the definition of inconsistencies, where different sections of a contract provide conflicting deadlines, the change introduces a direct contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 15. This Agreement supersedes any prior agreements and undertakings between the parties with respect to Propri etary Information supplied to each other , and is the complete agreement of the parties in relation to the subject matter of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: 15. This Agreement supersedes any prior agreements and undertakings between the parties with respect to Proprietary Information supplied to each other, except for any confidentiality agreements signed within the last year, and is the complete agreement of the parties in relation to the subject matter of this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: By adding 'except for any confidentiality agreements signed within the last year' to the clause stating this is the complete agreement, an in-text contradiction is created. It is now unclear whether prior agreements apply or not, leading to uncertainty and potential disputes.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Agreement supersedes any prior agreements and undertakings between the parties with respect to Propri etary Information supplied to each other , except for any confidentiality agreements signed within the last year, and is the complete agreement of the parties in relation to the subject matter of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces an exception ('except for any confidentiality agreements signed within the last year') to the original statement that 'This Agreement supersedes any prior agreements... and is the complete agreement'. This directly contradicts the claim of being the complete agreement, creating uncertainty about which agreements are in effect. The contradicted text and changed text are the same, demonstrating that the alteration causes an in-text contradiction. This aligns with the definition of 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction' because the different sections of the contract now provide conflicting information regarding which agreements are superseded.
